mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Null Hypothesis: Never Overlook Understanding Limits (H₀: No Observed Unusual Luck)

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 2
mnemonics.content: P-value: Probability of Perceiving Vanishingly small Evidence (if H₀ true)

mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 3
mnemonics.content: Type I & II Errors: I see a False Positive (reject true H₀), II miss a False Negative (fail to reject false H₀)

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: A test of significance assesses the evidence against a null hypothesis (H₀).

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The p-value is the probability of obtaining results as extreme as the observed data, assuming the null hypothesis is true.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: A result is statistically significant if the p-value is less than a predetermined significance level (α), leading to the rejection of H₀.
